channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE seeks access to taxpayer data at IRS: AP sources
item.description: Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency is seeking access to troves of sensitive taxpayer data at the IRS.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The firings come just weeks after an American Airlines flight collided mid-air with a military Black Hawk helicopter shortly before landing at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: A fired national park ranger lost his dream job. He says the public is losing more
item.description: Brian Gibbs was an environmental educator at Effigy Mounds National Monument in Iowa. His job was among those nixed by the Trump administration as it pushes to shrink federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How a South Texas community college embraced apprenticeships to ease a growing nursing shortage
item.description: South Texas College in McAllen launched one of the first registered nursing apprenticeships in the country as area hospitals expect the need for nurses to increase.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Sneha Dey, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine and Europe worry about being sidelined as Trump pushes direct talks with Russia on war's end
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s approach to ending Russia’s war against Ukraine has left European allies and Ukrainian officials worried they are being largely sidelined by the new U.S. administration as Washington and Moscow plan direct negotiations.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Europe tries to muscle up as U.S. plots mediation to help end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: European leaders are taking steps to answer assertive U.S. efforts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine. France on Sunday announced a meeting of continental leaders and Baltic countries called for new steps to strengthen Kyiv’s hand in any future talks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats' headaches multiply in battle for Senate
item.description: Correction: The dates of Sens. Warner's and Shaheen's last wins have been corrected. Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith’s (D) decision to not run for reelection in 2026 is adding another headache for Democrats trying to win back control of the Senate in two years.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo U.S. anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and host Canada.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Tenney’s press release noted that this proposed bill would “permanently codify a new federal holiday.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP budget resolution in jeopardy amid concerns from moderates
item.description: The House GOP’s budget resolution could be in jeopardy of not clearing the full chamber amid concerns from moderates over likely cuts to social safety net programs — particularly Medicaid — with a handful undecided on whether they will support the key measure. Reps. David Valadao (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Congressman urges Trump admin to help ailing Area 51 veterans
item.description: “What I saw was disturbing. I am angered,” Rep. Mark Takano, D-Calif., says about the plight of Area 51 vets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Staff at CDC and NIH are reeling as Trump administration cuts workforce
item.description: As many as 1,300 probationary employees at CDC and 1,500 at NIH are losing their jobs. Many fear for the future of public health and scientific research.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, Pien Huang, and Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: An open Minnesota Senate seat is enticing to plenty but unclear how many will take plunge
item.description: Minnesota has its first open-seat Senate race since 2006. Many candidates are giving it a look and both parties say they’re well-positioned to win.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Concerns over mass deportations lead to ‘Know Your Rights‘ discussion in Brooklyn Center
item.description: Amid concerns over the Trump Administration’s plans for mass deportations, nearly three dozen people who live in Brooklyn Park and Brooklyn Center attended a panel discussion Wednesday night to ask questions about how to respond if ICE shows up at a home or workplace, and also asked about the role o
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5
